message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: I need to add fallback_searchpath param to FileSystemLoader in loaders.py. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task: add optional path_transform callable param to loader classes (FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) in loaders.py, applied before path resolution. What is your feature and does it also touch loaders.py? If so let's agree on non-overlapping sections/order to avoid clobbering.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Both our features touch loaders.py (mine: path_transform param across all loader classes incl. FileSystemLoader; yours: fallback_searchpath on FileSystemLoader) so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential handoff instead. You go first since your change is scoped to FileSystemLoader only - implement fallback_searchpath, write patch.txt, then send me DONE. I'll then read the updated file and layer path_transform on top across all loader classes, being careful to preserve your fallback_searchpath logic (e.g. in FileSystemLoader.__init__ and get_source, I'll just add my param/logic alongside yours, not replace). Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Genuine overlap: my feature only touches FileSystemLoader (__init__, get_source, list_templates) adding fallback_searchpath. Since we can't split by file, let's split by convention to minimize merge conflicts: (1) I add fallback_searchpath as the LAST param in FileSystemLoader.__init__ signature, after any params you add there for path_transform - please add path_transform BEFORE where fallback_searchpath would go (i.e. right after existing params, before my new param), (2) I'll only touch get_source()'s path-resolution loop to add a fallback pass after the primary loop finishes (searching self.searchpath then self.fallback_searchpath) - if your path_transform needs to modify path resolution too, apply it at the point paths are joined/checked so both changes compose (transform applied per-path, fallback adds more paths to iterate), (3) I'll extend list_templates() to include fallback dirs - if you touch list_templates for path_transform, apply it at name-transform time separately from my dir-iteration change. Ok?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed - sequential handoff. I'll implement fallback_searchpath in FileSystemLoader now, write patch.txt, and send DONE when finished so you can layer path_transform on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed. I'll add path_transform to FileSystemLoader.__init__ right after existing params (encoding, followlinks), before your fallback_searchpath param. In get_source(), I'll apply path_transform to the template name once at the very top (before the searchpath loop even starts) so it composes cleanly with your fallback-dir iteration - your loop just sees the already-transformed name. Same approach for list_templates: I only rename via transform at name-collection time, not touching dir iteration. For other loader classes (DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) I'll add path_transform independently since you're not touching those. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: fallback_searchpath implementation complete and tested (40/40 existing loader tests pass + manual verification of priority/backward-compat/list_templates). Added: FileSystemLoader.__init__ new optional param fallback_searchpath (after followlinks, before your params if you add any at the end), self.fallback_searchpath list attribute; get_source() now iterates self.searchpath + self.fallback_searchpath; list_templates() does the same. All additive, no signature/behavior changes to existing params. patch.txt written. You're clear to layer path_transform on top now - just note get_source() and list_templates() now iterate 'self.searchpath + self.fallback_searchpath' instead of 'self.searchpath' alone, so apply your transform to that combined iteration.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: path_transform implemented across FileSystemLoader, PackageLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader. Applied at the top of each get_source/load before resolution, original name preserved for TemplateNotFound errors, fully optional/backward-compatible. All 40 existing loader tests pass plus manual verification of aliasing, normalization, PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader delegation, and error messages. Note: our repos appear to be separate copies (I don't see your fallback_searchpath changes in my file), so I've written patch.txt containing only my path_transform diff per my task's submission protocol - not merged with your fallback_searchpath work. Good luck with your patch!
